rulings.Case Title: CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC., A COLORADO CORPORATION VS. ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY ANAD COUNTY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate And/Or Writ Of Adminstrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 5, PETITIONER CP III RINCON TOWERS, INC.'s Petition For Writ Of Mandate And/Or Writ Of Adminstrative Mandate. Denied. Petitioner's assertion that Respondent's decision is not supported by any "explanation or findings of any kind" is incorrect. (Petition, 28. See also, id. at 36.) The Petition and Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed on February 11, 2014 and February 20, 2014, respectively, neither contemplate nor address Respondent's Findings of Fact and Statement of Decision issued on February 19, 2014. (See Notice of Lodgment of Supplemental Administrative Record, filed on Mar. 10, 2014, Exhibit A.) The Petition predates the Findings of Fact and Statement of Decision and is predicated on the decision letter dated November 22, 2013. (Points & Auth., p. p. 1:17-18, p. 5, fn. 5.) Within 5 days, Petitioner is directed to pick up the copies of the administrative record lodged with Department 302 and may contact the court clerk, (415) 551-3723, to arrange the pick up. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. In ruling on a demurrer, the complaint must be "liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP sec. 452.) A general demurrer should be sustained "only if the complaint fails to state a cause of action under any possible legal theory." (Sheenhan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) 45 Cal.4th 992, 998.) Labor Code sec. 4558(c) requires the plaintiff to prove that the manufacturer of the power press conveyed the necessity of the guards to the employer. The Fourth Amended Complaint ("FAC") pleads that the manufacturer "directly or indirectly" notified Defendant about the need for the guard. (FAC, par. 22.) The FAC also pleads that the Use and Operations Manual contained information about the necessity of the guard. (FAC, par. 22.) Liberally construing the allegations, the FAC states a cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MERVYN SILBERBERG VS. UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Defts To Provide Verified Presponses Withour Objection, Together With Identificationa Dn Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MERVYN SILBERBERG Notice Of Moiton And Motion To Compel Defts To Provide Verified Presponses Withour Objection, Together With Identificationa Dn Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff MERVYN SILBERBERG's Motion to Compel is DENIED as to the Request for Production of Documents, Set 3, Form Interrogatories, Set 3, and Request for Admission, Set Two for failure to meet requirements of C.C.P. § 2016.040. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's request for sanctions is under submission Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 88 TOWNSEND STREET OWNERS ASOCIATION, VS. 699 SECOND DEVELOPMENT LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Seeking Leave File And Serve Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF 88 TOWNSEND STREET OWNERS ASOCIATION's Motion Seeking Leave To File And Serve Second Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiff may file its proposed Second Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. There is an established policy that "trial courts are to liberally permit¿amendments, at any stage of the proceeding." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.) Defendants have not established that Plaintiff was unreasonably dilatory in seeking the amendment or that the amendment would be unduly prejudicial. Defendants' request for a 120 day trial continuance is denied at this time. Should any party wish to move the trial date, a motion may be filed in Dept. 206. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: G. PAUL WILLIS VS. THOMAS A. LUCAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT THOMAS LUCAS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrers to the first and second causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff apparently relies on Probate Code sec. 6451(a) to support his assertion that the adoption severed the parent/child relationship. The amended complaint, however, only refers to Probate Code sec. 6451(b). Plaintiff should plead the correct code provision. The first and second causes of action have sufficient facts and plaintiff adequately alleges a duty to disclose. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to allege a recognized cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WESTERN PROPERTY ADVISORS, INC. VS. L.F. GEORGE PROPERTIES CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTs L.F. GEORGE PROPERTIES CORP, JUDAH TERRACES LLC, 6901 GEARY BLVD LLC, GEORGE LAM's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. Defendants seek relief from default on the grounds that George Lam does not speak English fluently, and therefore did not realize he was being sued until his assistant returned to work and translated the documents. Yet, the only evidence submitted in opposition is a declaration from Mr. Lam written in English. No translator's oath is submitted, as required by Evidence Code sec. 751(c). As such, there is no credible evidence that would justify setting aside the default under CCP 473(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
